stem: A zip line cable stretches 255 feet from the top of a hill to a platform below, descending at a 28-degree angle from the horizontal. Given that sin 28° ≈ 8/17, cos 28° ≈ 15/17, and tan 28° ≈ 8/15, what is the vertical drop of the zip line, in feet?
explanation: The cable is the hypotenuse (255 feet) and the vertical drop is the side opposite the 28° angle. Using sin 28° = opposite/hypotenuse: vertical drop = 255 × sin 28° = 255 × (8/17) = 2040/17 = 120 feet.

distractorRationale.A: Used tan instead of sin, confusing the trig ratios: 255 × (8/15) = 2040/15 = 136.
distractorRationale.C: Confused 28° with 30° and applied the 30-60-90 rule (short leg = hypotenuse/2): 255 × (1/2) = 127.5.
distractorRationale.D: Used cos instead of sin, finding the horizontal distance instead of the vertical drop: 255 × (15/17) = 225.

imageDescription: A right triangle showing a ski gondola cable as the hypotenuse labeled 255 ft, stretching from a base station at the bottom to an upper station at the top. The angle between the cable and the horizontal ground is labeled 28°. The vertical rise on the right side is marked with a question mark.
